title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Execution Trace: Response Commit Order
summary: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the response commit order increment in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Response Commit Order · Theory Response Commit Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 7840 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
